developer_response.body: Hey! Would you be able to tell me what version of Quest you're playing on?

reviews.review_description:
Hey! You probably don't remember me, but a while back in Orion Drift, I told you my grandma loves dreams about flying and how she would love to play this game. We finally played it, but unfortunately, the recording didn't come out well. Overall, she loved it! She thought the mechanics were great, the sprite was funny (to her), and that it was overall amazing. However, she did have one issue with the game, being that it wasn't exactly clear where she could go, or what she should do.

MY TIPS TO MAKE THE GAME BETTER: 
Add signs similar to gorilla tag so you know where everything is, and what everything is called. 

Make everything more closed off. What I mean by this is add walls around all of the important stuff, but keep the roof open, so if you want, you can just fly away. 

Remove proximity chat, I would commonly notice that if I get too far away from people, I can't hear them, which would make sense for a game like Gorilla Tag, but this game is so fast, and you can easily fly away from everything and everyone to do whatever you want, so removing proximity chat would make it easier.

(This is just an idea, you don't have to consider this one) Make there a party system so that you can grab onto people, that way they can't really get away. 


That's all my ideas. Overall, this game is AMAZING. Keep up the updates!

The community is super nice. The devs and mods are even nicer. The fact this game has just been released and￼ it is this good I can’t wait to see what comes in the future 100% play this if you can (THIS WAS NOT WRITTEN BY AI) I am a real person👍👍

reviews.review_title: Balloon Bats the fun flying experience
reviews.review_description:
Balloon Bats: A 50-Year-Old’s Wild Ride Review

Who says VR games are just for the young? Balloon Bats is a delightful little gem that had this 50-year-old flapping, soaring, and laughing like I was back in my Saturday morning cartoon days. The concept is simple but oh-so-satisfying: you play as a bat, dodging obstacles and chasing balloons in a colorful, whimsical world. It’s like being part of a fantasy dreamscape where you get to defy gravity—without needing a cape!

What really struck me was how immersive it felt. The mechanics are smooth, the visuals are engaging with some references to Brittany, and the sound effects add just the right level of charm. Plus, there’s something oddly therapeutic about popping balloons mid-flight—it’s like stress relief with wings!

Sure, it’s aimed at the younger crowd, but there’s no age limit on fun. I found myself competing with my younger family members, and let’s just say, i can see a difference in skills :-; but i did better than my wife… My arms might be sore from all the flapping, but it’s a small price to pay for the sheer joy Balloon Bats brought me.

If you’re a 50-year-old looking for a fun way to shake up your routine or bond with the younger generation, strap on that VR headset. Who knew bats and balloons would make such a fantastic combo? 10/10, would pop balloons again!

Today I was zooming across Orion drift when this guy comes up to me and says " do you want to join my new game balloon bats?" So I say Sure why not! First of all Great advertising. When we loaded into the game with about 6 people or so, we got to meet the creator and one of the moderators named chip. They were both very nice and helpful. In this game you get to flap your "wings" just like a bat! There are modes like FFA or "free for all" where you have to pop balloons by finding them across the map or popping other people's above their heads first one to 10 wins. Overall this is a great game would recommend. 5 stars easily.
